categories.title: YEARS IN HISTORY
categories.theme: Landmark historical events — answer is the YEAR the event occurred

clues.clue: World War I began in this year, setting off one of the deadliest conflicts in human history.
Christopher Columbus first made landfall in the Bahamas in this year, changing the course of world history.
The Wall Street Crash, triggering the Great Depression, occurred on October 24th of this year.
The Spanish Civil War erupted in this year, eventually bringing Francisco Franco to power.
The USSR detonated its first atomic bomb in this year, shocking the Western world and escalating the Cold War.

categories.title: WORLD WAR II MILESTONES
categories.theme: Key events, people, and places of the Second World War — answer is the PERSON, PLACE, or THING

clues.clue: This mustachioed German dictator led the Nazi regime that plunged Europe into war.
Britain and France declared war on Germany in 1939 after it invaded this Eastern European country.
From this French port, roughly 200,000 British troops were evacuated in 1940 in a desperate retreat.
This naval engagement in the Pacific, fought in June 1942, is widely considered the turning point of the war against Japan.
This Baltic seaport served as Nazi Germany's primary rocket research and development centre throughout the war.

finals.title: DEFENESTRATION OF PRAGUE
finals.clue: This act — Protestant nobles in Prague hurling Catholic Lords Regents from a castle window in 1618 — sparked the Bohemian Revolt and helped ignite a devastating European war that would last thirty years. What did those Protestants do to their lords?
finals.answer: Threw them out of a window

finals.title: FIRST PRIME MINISTER
finals.clue: When Pakistan was created as an independent nation in 1947, this man became its very first Prime Minister, serving until his assassination in 1951.
